content.full_text:
Eusebio Sarte                                    October 15, 1976
Political Reform Division
Secretary of State's Office
111 Capitol Mall


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSIS DIVISION

Lobbyist Reporting


I have reviewed your memo of September 16, 1976 and the attached
letter from Mr. Robert N. Lowry. It is apparent that you may
have relied on my memo of June 1, 1976. In that memo I stated,
"Each monthly report must disclose all authorized employers
regardless of payments received." Since clients generally do
not make payments directly to the lobbyist, it was my intention
that this requirement would pertain only to those employers who
made payments directly to lobbyists.

I believe Mr. Lowry's interpretation is correct and that he need
only report a client in the month in which he engages in lobbyist
activity for that client (PRT Regulation 5-619(e)). He must,
however, disclose his immediate employer (the law firm) each month
regardless of payments received (Government Code Section 86107(a)).

I hope this clarifies the situation for you. Attached is a copy
of my response to Mr. Lowry.

Please contact me if I can be of further assistance.


J. RICHARD EICHMAN
Special Compliance Representative

cc: David Pitman
     Robert Summers

[illegible] of [illegible]

[illegible text at top of page]


[illegible] would [illegible]

[Several paragraphs of faint, illegible text]

[illegible] [illegible] of
[illegible] [illegible]

October 15, 1976


Mr. Robert N. Lowry
Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison
Attorneys at Law
One Eleven Sutter Street
San Francisco, California  94104

Dear Mr. Lowry:

Mr. Sarte of the Office of the Secretary of State has asked me to respond to your letter of October 9, 1976.  His letter to you dated August 26, 1976 resulted from a misunderstanding between the Fair Political Practices Commission and the Secretary of State's office.

Your interpretation of Regulation 18619(e) is correct.  Additionally, please note that you must disclose Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison on each monthly report regardless of payments received.

Sincerely,


J. RICHARD EICHMAN
Special Compliance Representative

State of California

Memorandum

To : Richard Eichman
     Fair Political Practices Commission

Date: September 16, 1976

From : Secretary of State
        E. Sarte

Subject: Interpretation of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619(e)

Attached is a letter from Mr. Robert N. Lowry of the law firm,
Brobeck, Phleger and Harrison, which is self-explanatory.

It was my understanding that Title 2, Cal. Adm. Code Section
18619(e) requires the lobbyist to disclose in his periodic
reports all the names of each of the persons who have hired the
business entity of which he is a member and for whom he was
engaged to lobby regardless as to whether or not any lobbying
activity was performed on their behalf during the period covered
by the report.

Mr. Lowry has a different interpretation of the above cited
section of the code which was stated in the letter.

Please clarify us on this matter.

Sincerely,

[signature]
EUSEBIO I. SARTE
Political Reform Division

cc: Mr. Richard N. Lowry

O;93
dimela time

[The rest of the document appears too faint or illegible to accurately transcribe]

State of California

Memorandum

To    :  John Keplinger, Chief
         Technical Assistance
         & Analysis Division
         Fair Political Practices Commission

Date:  September 24, 1976

From  : Secretary of State

Subject:  Lobbyist Reporting


         The attached letter raises an interpretive question regarding
         lobbyist reporting.

         I understand this question has been raised by others and would
         appreciate clarification of the regulation in question.


                                            DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
                                            Political Reform Division

DBP/cs
att.

cc:  T. Miller
     E. Sarte

o 9 7
ar'il ee 2 ll we

BROBECK, PHLEGER & HARRISON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
ONE ELEVEN SUTTER STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94104
(415) 434-0900

TELEPHONE
(415) 434-0900

RECEIVED AND FILED
In the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California

SEP 1 7 1976

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

September 10, 1976

Mr. Eusebio I. Sarte, Auditor
Political Reform Division
Office of the Secretary of State
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

Re: Form 625 - Lobbyist Report

Dear Mr. Sarte:

I have your letter of August 26, 1976 with
respect to my Form 625 Lobbyist Report for the month of
June 1976 executed July 29, 1976.

Your letter points out that the report should
disclose the names of the clients who have retained
Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison for whom I am lobbying,
citing 2 Cal.Adm. Code §18619(e).

That section of the Regulations provides in part
that the filer shall

"disclose in his periodic report the names
of each of the persons who have hired the
business entity of which he is a member or
employee and for whom he has engaged in
any lobbying activity during the period...."
(Emphasis supplied)

The underscored text of this regulation appears
to require listing of the persons who have retained the firm

BROBECK, PHLEGER & HARRISON

Mr. Eusebio I. Sarte
September 10, 1976

2.

only to the extent that the filer has engaged in lobbying
activity on their behalf during the period.

As I did not engage in lobbying activity for any
person during June, the period covered by the report, it
does not appear that §18619(e) requires me to list the
names of any persons who may have retained the firm.

If this is not a correct interpretation of the
regulation, please so advise me.

Very truly yours,

Robert N. Lowry

RNL:SL

/
